metadata.document_title: Software Requirements Specification — Live Proctoring Dashboard
metadata.organization: African University College of Digital Technologies (AUCDT)

1_1_purpose.description: This SRS defines the functional and non-functional requirements for the Live Proctoring Dashboard — a real-time web-based application that enables human proctors and AI systems to monitor, flag, and manage online examinees simultaneously. The document serves as the authoritative contract between stakeholders, designers, and developers.

1_2_scope.summary: LPD is a browser-based dashboard that streams live video feeds from remote examinees, applies AI-driven anomaly detection (multi-face, tab-switch, audio, identity), surfaces real-time alerts, and provides proctor controls — all within a single, responsive interface.
in_scope: AI-generated behavioral alert classification and surfacing
Session and exam management (start, pause, terminate)
Audit log generation per exam session
out_of_scope: Student identity enrollment (handled by upstream IDV service)

1_3_definitions_acronyms_abbreviations.definition: Web Real-Time Communication protocol for browser-based video streaming
Multi-Face Alert — triggered when >1 face is detected in an examinee's video frame
Tab Switch Alert — triggered when an examinee navigates away from the exam tab
Region of Interest within a video frame

1_4_references: IEEE Std 830-1998, IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018, Systems and software engineering — Requirements engineering
section_1_introduction.1_5_overview: Section 2 provides overall system description and constraints. Section 3 enumerates specific functional requirements organized by feature. Section 4 covers non-functional requirements. Section 5 describes external interface requirements.

2_1_product_perspective.context: LPD is a subsystem of the AUCDT Utilities Platform. It consumes streams from an upstream Media Server, alert events from an AI Inference Engine, and session data from the Exam Management Service, then presents a unified proctor-facing UI.
system_interfaces: Media Server — WebRTC/WHIP for inbound student video streams
AI Inference Engine — REST/WebSocket for real-time alert events
Exam Management Service — REST API for session CRUD
Identity Verification Service — REST for face-match scores
Audit Log Service — event-driven (Kafka/WebSocket) for immutable records
Notification Service — push alerts to proctor browser

context_diagram.note: Logical data flow: Student Clients → Media Server → LPD (video tiles) | AI Engine → LPD (alert badges) | Proctor → LPD → Exam Mgmt Service (interventions)

2_2_product_functions: F-01: Real-time multi-feed video grid display
F-02: AI alert ingestion and visual surfacing
F-04: Session-level controls (start, pause, end exam)
F-05: System health and live-count status bar
F-06: Alert history and audit log viewer
F-07: Role-based access for Proctor, Senior Proctor, and Admin
F-08: Configurable alert threshold and notification preferences
F-09: Zoom-in / focus mode for individual examinee feed

2_3_user_classes_and_characteristics.description: Monitors assigned examinees in real-time. Can warn, flag, or eject students. Primary day-to-day user.
2_3_user_classes_and_characteristics.frequency: High — active during all exam sessions

2_3_user_classes_and_characteristics.description: Oversees multiple proctors, can override decisions, access all exam rooms.
Configures thresholds, manages user accounts, reviews audit logs.
Not a direct LPD user but is the subject of observation. Their browser client streams video.

client.network: Minimum 10 Mbps downstream per 10 concurrent feeds

server.database: PostgreSQL 15+ for session data, Redis for ephemeral state

2_5_design_and_implementation_constraint: All video streams must use end-to-end encryption (DTLS-SRTP via WebRTC)
No video frame data may be persisted without explicit institutional consent
WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility compliance required for all UI components
Maximum dashboard JavaScript bundle size: 500 KB gzipped
System must operate within GDPR and FERPA data handling requirements
All proctor interventions must generate immutable audit log entries
Frontend must be built with React 19 / TypeScript / Tailwind CSS per platform standards
2_6_assumptions_and_dependencies: AI Inference Engine API is available and provides alert events via WebSocket
Media Server supports WebRTC WHIP/WHEP protocols
Institutions have obtained required consent from examinees for video monitoring
Proctor workstations have hardware-accelerated video decoding
Network infrastructure supports required bandwidth per concurrent session count

FR-01_video_grid.title: Real-Time Multi-Feed Video Grid
FR-01_video_grid.description: The system shall display a responsive grid of live video tiles, each representing one active examinee.

sub_requirements.statement: The grid SHALL support 1 to 64 simultaneous video tiles in a single viewport.
sub_requirements.rationale: Supports small to large exam cohorts without requiring separate views.

sub_requirements.statement: Each tile SHALL display: live video feed, examinee name, student ID, exam ID, and an active/inactive status indicator.
sub_requirements.rationale: Proctor needs identity context at a glance (as shown in dashboard screenshot).

sub_requirements.statement: Each tile SHALL display a colored status badge (green = normal, amber = warning, red = flagged) derived from the latest AI alert severity.
sub_requirements.rationale: Enables rapid triage across many simultaneous feeds.

sub_requirements.statement: The grid layout SHALL automatically reflow when tiles are added or removed without page reload.
A proctor SHALL be able to click any tile to enter Focus Mode (FR-09), enlarging that feed to 75% of viewport.
sub_requirements.rationale: Enables close inspection of a suspicious examinee.

sub_requirements.statement: Video tiles SHALL render at a minimum of 15 fps under normal network conditions.
sub_requirements.rationale: Below 15 fps proctors miss rapid behavioral events.

FR-02_alert_system.title: AI Alert Ingestion and Display
FR-02_alert_system.description: The system shall receive, classify, and visually surface behavioral anomaly alerts generated by the AI Inference Engine.

alert_types.label: Multi-Face Alert
alert_types.trigger: AI detects more than one face in the examinee's video frame

alert_types.label: Tab Switch Alert
alert_types.trigger: Browser focus/visibility events indicate examinee navigated away from exam tab

alert_types.label: No Face Detected
alert_types.trigger: AI detects no face present for > 5 consecutive seconds

alert_types.label: Suspicious Audio
alert_types.trigger: Audio analysis detects secondary voice or disallowed communication

alert_types.label: Phone/Device Detected
alert_types.trigger: Object detection identifies unauthorized device in frame

alert_types.label: Identity Mismatch
alert_types.trigger: Face match score against enrolled ID drops below threshold

sub_requirements.statement: The dashboard SHALL subscribe to the AI Inference Engine via WebSocket and process alert events within 500 ms of generation.
Active alerts SHALL be surfaced as notification cards in a persistent alert tray at the bottom of the dashboard viewport, as shown in the reference design.
Alert cards SHALL include: alert type label, severity badge, student segment ID, exam ID, and timestamp.
Unacknowledged CRITICAL or HIGH alerts SHALL trigger a browser notification and an audible chime (dismissible in settings).
A proctor SHALL be able to acknowledge or dismiss an alert; dismissed alerts SHALL be retained in the session audit log.
Alert card for Tab Switch SHALL use red color (#ef4444) and shield icon to visually distinguish from Multi-Face amber (#f59e0b).

FR-03_proctor_interventions.title: Proctor Intervention Controls
FR-03_proctor_interventions.description: The system shall provide per-examinee action controls accessible from the video tile context menu or Focus Mode panel.

actions.description: Send an on-screen text warning to the examinee
Open a private text chat channel with the examinee
Mark submission for post-exam review; does not interrupt exam
Terminate examinee session and lock their exam submission
Prompt examinee to hold ID document up to camera for re-verification

sub_requirements.statement: EJECT action SHALL require a two-step confirmation dialog before execution.
All intervention actions SHALL be logged to the Audit Log Service with proctor ID, timestamp, examinee ID, action type, and optional notes.
Proctor role SHALL NOT be able to perform EJECT without Senior Proctor approval if institution policy flag `require_senior_approval_for_eject` is enabled.

FR-04_session_management.title: Exam Session Management
FR-04_session_management.description: The system shall allow authorized users to manage the lifecycle of proctored exam sessions.

sub_requirements.statement: The dashboard header SHALL display: session name, exam ID, scheduled duration, elapsed time, and live examinee count.
Authorized users SHALL be able to: start a scheduled session, extend session duration, pause all feeds, and end the session.
Session state transitions SHALL be: SCHEDULED → ACTIVE → PAUSED → ENDED. No backward transitions except PAUSED → ACTIVE.
When a session ends, the dashboard SHALL display a summary modal: total examinees, total alerts by type, flagged count, ejected count.

FR-05_system_health.title: System Operational Status Indicator
FR-05_system_health.description: The dashboard shall display a real-time system health indicator reflecting infrastructure status and live examinee count.

sub_requirements.statement: A status chip SHALL appear in the top-right of the dashboard showing one of: 'Active' (green), 'Degraded' (amber), or 'Offline' (red).
The status bar SHALL display total live examinee count (e.g., '1,247 Live') updated every 5 seconds.
On 'Degraded' or 'Offline' status, the system SHALL display a banner explaining the issue and estimated resolution time if available.

FR-06_audit_log.title: Audit Log and Session Report
FR-06_audit_log.description: The system shall generate tamper-evident, timestamped logs of all alerts and proctor actions for compliance and dispute resolution.

sub_requirements.statement: Every AI alert, proctor action, and session state change SHALL produce an immutable log entry.
Proctors SHALL be able to view the alert history for any examinee within the current session via a side panel.
Admins SHALL be able to export a full session report as PDF or JSON, containing: session metadata, per-examinee alert timeline, and all interventions.
Audit logs SHALL be retained for a minimum of 7 years per institutional compliance requirements.

FR-07_rbac.title: Role-Based Access Control
FR-07_rbac.description: The system shall enforce RBAC with three roles: Proctor, Senior Proctor, and Admin.

sub_requirements.statement: Authentication SHALL use JWT tokens with a maximum lifetime of 8 hours.
Unauthorized access attempts SHALL be logged and trigger a security alert to the Admin.

FR-08_alert_configuration.title: Alert Threshold Configuration
FR-08_alert_configuration.description: Admin users shall be able to configure per-institution alert sensitivity thresholds.

configurable_parameters.description: Minimum AI confidence to raise a Multi-Face Alert
Seconds with no face detected before alert fires
Grace period before Tab Switch Alert triggers
dB level triggering suspicious audio alert

FR-09_focus_mode.title: Focus Mode — Single Examinee View
FR-09_focus_mode.description: The system shall provide an expanded single-examinee view for detailed inspection.

sub_requirements.statement: Focus Mode SHALL display: enlarged video feed, full alert history timeline for the session, AI confidence scores for active detections, and all intervention controls.
Entering Focus Mode SHALL NOT interrupt monitoring of other tiles in the background grid.
Focus Mode SHALL be dismissible via Escape key or a close button, returning to the full grid.

FR-10_export.title: Session Report Export
FR-10_export.description: Senior Proctors and Admins shall be able to export session reports.
report_contents: Session metadata (exam name, date, duration, institution)
Per-examinee table: student ID, name, alert count by type, flags, final disposition
All proctor interventions with actor and timestamp

3_2_use_cases.name: Proctor Responds to Multi-Face Alert
3_2_use_cases.preconditions: Exam session is ACTIVE; proctor is authenticated with Proctor role
main_flow: 1. AI Engine detects two faces in Examinee S2's video frame with 87% confidence
2. System receives WebSocket event ALERT_MFA for S2/Exam 1247
3. System displays amber alert card 'Multi-Face Alert — S2 • Exam 1247' in alert tray
4. Examinee S2's tile border turns amber
5. Browser emits audible chime and browser notification
6. Proctor clicks alert card → opens Focus Mode for S2
7. Proctor reviews enlarged feed; confirms secondary face is present
8. Proctor clicks 'Warn' → types warning message → confirms
9. System sends on-screen warning to S2 and logs the intervention
10. Proctor clicks 'Flag' to mark for post-exam review
11. Proctor dismisses Focus Mode; alert card remains until acknowledged
alternate_flows: 6a. Proctor determines it was a false positive → dismisses alert → logs false_positive reason
3_2_use_cases.postconditions: Intervention logged; alert marked as reviewed; S2 continues exam

3_2_use_cases.name: Proctor Responds to Tab Switch Alert
main_flow: 1. Examinee S3 switches browser tabs during Exam 1248
2. Examinee client fires visibilitychange event; backend records TSA
3. System displays red alert card 'Tab Switch Alert — S3 • Exam 1248'
4. S3's tile border turns red
5. Proctor reviews; this is S3's second tab switch event this session
6. Proctor clicks 'Warn' with message 'Stay on exam tab or session will be terminated'
7. Proctor flags S3 for review
3_2_use_cases.postconditions: Warning delivered; flag set; audit log updated

3_2_use_cases.name: Admin Views System Operational Status
main_flow: 1. Admin opens dashboard
2. System displays 'Active' status chip and '1,247 Live' counter
3. Admin verifies all subsystems healthy via health panel
4. Counter auto-refreshes every 5 seconds
3_2_use_cases.postconditions: Admin has verified system operational status

4_1_performance.requirement: Glass-to-glass video latency SHALL be ≤ 500 ms under normal network conditions (≥ 10 Mbps).
AI alert events SHALL appear on the proctor dashboard within 500 ms of AI engine generation.
Initial dashboard load with 16 tiles SHALL complete within 3 seconds on a 10 Mbps connection.
The system SHALL support 1,500 concurrent live examinee sessions per deployment instance.
A single proctor SHALL be able to monitor up to 32 examinees per session without performance degradation.

4_2_reliability.requirement: LPD SHALL achieve 99.9% uptime during scheduled exam windows (SLA).
Loss of a single AI alert event SHALL NOT crash the dashboard; the system SHALL queue and retry missed events.
On WebSocket disconnection, the client SHALL automatically reconnect with exponential backoff (max 30 s) and display a degraded-state banner.
Audit log entries SHALL be written atomically; partial writes SHALL be rolled back.

4_3_security.requirement: All video streams SHALL use DTLS-SRTP. All API and WebSocket traffic SHALL use TLS 1.3.
All dashboard access SHALL require JWT authentication with RS256 signing.
All API endpoints SHALL enforce RBAC. Attempting a forbidden action SHALL return HTTP 403 and be logged.
No raw video frames SHALL be stored server-side without explicit examinee consent and institutional data processing agreement.
Dashboard sessions SHALL invalidate on browser close or 30-minute inactivity.
The system SHALL undergo annual third-party penetration testing; critical findings SHALL be remediated within 30 days.

4_4_usability.requirement: UI SHALL comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA. All alert cards SHALL be screen-reader accessible with ARIA labels.
A new proctor with basic computer literacy SHALL be able to perform core monitoring tasks after a 30-minute onboarding session.
All user action failures SHALL display a human-readable error message within 1 second.
Dashboard SHALL be fully functional on screens from 1366×768 to 4K resolution.

4_5_maintainability.requirement: UI components SHALL be independently testable React components with unit test coverage ≥ 80%.
All client-side errors SHALL be forwarded to a centralized logging service with structured JSON logs.
The frontend SHALL be deployable via Docker image with zero manual configuration steps.

4_6_compliance.requirement: Processing of biometric video data SHALL comply with GDPR Article 9; lawful basis and DPA required per institution.
Student examination records surfaced in LPD SHALL be treated as education records under FERPA.
Audit logs SHALL be retained for a minimum of 7 years; video data (if stored) per institutional policy, max 2 years.

elements: Per-tile: video feed, name overlay, student ID, exam ID, status badge (green/amber/red)
Tile context menu (right-click or kebab icon)
Alert cards (icon + label + student segment + exam ID)
Card color coding per alert type
UI-03_alert_tray.reference: Matches bottom section of provided screenshot

UI-04_focus_mode_panel.trigger: Click on tile or alert card
elements: Intervention action buttons (Warn, Chat, Flag, Eject, Request ID)

5_2_software_interfaces.description: Dashboard subscribes to named video streams identified by student_segment_id
Dashboard emits structured log events for every proctor action

5_3_communication_interfaces.real_time_events: WebSocket persistent connection with heartbeat ping every 30 s

transitions: ACTIVE → ESCALATED (proctor clicks Escalate to Senior)

frontend.state_management: React Context + useReducer (or Zustand for alert queue)
frontend.webrtc_client: Native WebRTC API + WHEP client library

appendix_c_technology_recommendations.rationale: Aligns with existing AUCDT platform stack defined in CLAUDE.md

appendix_d_open_questions.question: Should the dashboard support simultaneous multi-session monitoring (proctor oversees two exam rooms at once)?
What is the maximum acceptable false-positive rate for MFA alerts before institutions consider the system unreliable?
Is clip recording (short video snippet on alert trigger) required, and if so, what are the consent and retention policies?
Should the examinee client expose a network quality indicator to the proctor tile?
